title: The Weekly Challenge - 209
task1_desc: You are given an array of binary bits that ends with 0.
task1_body: You are given an array of binary bits that ends with 0. Valid sequences in the bit string are: [0] -decodes-to-> "a" [1, 0] -> "b" [1, 1] -> "c" Write a script to print 1 if the last character is an "a" otherwise print 0. #### Example 1 Input: @bits = (1, 0, 0) Output: 1 The given array bits can be decoded as 2-bits character (10) followed by 1-bit character (0). #### Example 2 Input: @bits = (1, 1, 1, 0) Output: 0 Possible decode can be 2-bits character (11) followed by 2-bits character (10) i.e. the last character is not 1-bit character.
task2_desc: You are given an array of accounts i.e. name with list of email addresses.
task2_body:
You are given an array of accounts i.e. name with list of email addresses. Write a script to merge the accounts where possible. The accounts can only be merged if they have at least one email address in common. Example 1: Input: @accounts = [ ["A", "a1@a.com", "a2@a.com"], ["B", "b1@b.com"], ["A", "a3@a.com", "a1@a.com"] ] ] Output: [ ["A", "a1@a.com", "a2@a.com", "a3@a.com"], ["B", "b1@b.com"] ] Example 2: Input: @accounts = [ ["A", "a1@a.com", "a2@a.com"], ["B", "b1@b.com"], ["A", "a3@a.com"], ["B", "b2@b.com", "b1@b.com"] ] Output: [ ["A", "a1@a.com", "a2@a.com"], ["A", "a3@a.com"], ["B", "b1@b.com", "b2@b.com"] ]
Last date to submit the solution **23:59 (UK Time) Sunday 26th March 2023**.
